title: Rondo 4v1
instructions: Set up a 10x10 yard square with cones.
Four players stand on the outside, one defender in the middle.
Outside players must pass the ball around, keeping possession.
The defender tries to intercept or win the ball.
If the defender wins the ball, the player who made the bad pass becomes the defender.
Two-touch maximum for outside players.
coachingPoints: Open body shape to see all passing options.
Play the way you face when under pressure.
Quick ball movement — don't hold onto the ball.
Communicate with teammates.
variations: One-touch only for advanced players.
Increase to 5v2 for more challenge.
Expand or shrink the grid to adjust difficulty.

title: Wall Pass & Shoot
instructions: Player A starts with the ball 30 yards from goal.
Player B stands 10 yards ahead as the wall passer.
Player A passes to Player B and makes a run into the box.
Player B lays the ball off first-time into Player A's path.
Player A shoots first-time or with one touch.
Rotate roles after each attempt.
coachingPoints: Weight of the pass must be firm enough to play one-touch.
Timing of the run — don't go too early.
Shoot across the goalkeeper to the far post.
Follow up for rebounds.
variations: Add a passive defender to create pressure.
Alternate sides (left and right).
Require weak-foot finish.

title: 1v1 Dribble Gates
instructions: Set up 4 small gates (2 yards wide) in a 20x20 yard area.
Players pair up — one attacker, one defender.
Attacker scores a point by dribbling through any gate.
Defender tries to win the ball or force the attacker away from gates.
Play for 1 minute, then switch roles.
Keep score — most points wins.
coachingPoints: Keep the ball close and use changes of direction.
Use body feints to unbalance the defender.
Scan for open gates before committing.
Accelerate through the gate once you've beaten the defender.
variations: Add more gates for beginners.
Reduce the area for more intensity.
Allow defender to score by dribbling through gates if they win the ball.

title: Pressing Triggers 6v4
instructions: Mark out a 40x30 yard zone split into two halves.
6 attacking players (blue) try to keep possession across both halves.
4 defenders (red) press and try to win the ball.
Pressing trigger: when a back-pass is made, defenders sprint to close down.
If defenders win the ball, they try to score through mini goals.
Play for 3 minutes, then rotate defenders.
coachingPoints: Identify the pressing trigger — backward or sideways pass.
Press as a unit, not individually.
Cover shadow: cut off the next passing lane while pressing the ball.
Recover quickly if the press is broken.
variations: Change to 6v3 for easier pressing.
Add a neutral player who plays for the team in possession.
Require 8 passes for a point to increase pressing urgency.

title: Shuttle Runs with Ball
instructions: Set up cones at 5, 10, 15, and 20 yards from the start line.
Players dribble to the 5-yard cone, turn, and dribble back.
Then dribble to 10-yard cone and back, 15 and back, 20 and back.
Rest for 30 seconds.
Repeat for 4 sets.
coachingPoints: Keep the ball under close control during turns.
Use the sole of the foot to stop and turn quickly.
Maintain a high tempo throughout each set.
Focus on clean turns, not just speed.
variations: Without the ball for pure fitness.
Add a pass to a partner at each cone.
Time each set and try to beat your personal best.

title: Triangular Passing Pattern
instructions: Set up three cones in a triangle, 15 yards apart.
Two players at each cone.
Player A passes to Player B, then follows the pass to B's cone.
Player B receives, opens up, and passes to Player C, then follows.
Pattern continues — always follow your pass.
Increase speed gradually.
coachingPoints: Receive on the back foot to open your body.
Pass with pace — firm, driven passes along the ground.
Check your shoulder before receiving.
Sprint to the next cone after passing.
variations: Add a one-two before passing to the next station.
Play one-touch only.
Add a fourth cone to create a diamond pattern.

title: Finishing from Crosses
instructions: Set up with a full-size goal and a goalkeeper.
Wide player starts on the right wing with a supply of balls.
Two attackers start at the edge of the box.
Wide player dribbles to the byline and crosses.
Attackers make near-post and far-post runs to finish.
Add a defender after 5 minutes to create realistic pressure.
coachingPoints: Stagger runs — near post first, far post second.
Attack the ball aggressively — don't wait for it.
Aim for the target on first contact.
Far-post runner should arrive slightly later.
variations: Cross from the left side.
Add cutback crosses from the byline.
Include a midfielder arriving late for knock-downs.

title: Skill Dribble Course
instructions: Set up 8 cones in a zigzag pattern, 3 yards apart.
Dribble through all cones using the inside and outside of the foot.
At the last cone, turn and dribble back.
Focus on technique first, then increase speed.
Complete 5 full runs.
coachingPoints: Keep the ball close — small touches.
Use both feet throughout the course.
Keep your head up as much as possible.
Bend the knees and stay low for balance.
variations: Use only the weak foot.
Add a step-over at each cone.
Race against a partner for competition.

title: Back Line Shifting
instructions: Four defenders form a back line across the pitch.
Four attackers pass the ball across the front.
Defenders must shift as a unit following the ball.
When the ball goes wide, the nearest defender presses.
Remaining defenders tuck in and cover.
Coach calls 'play' — attackers try to penetrate with a through ball.
coachingPoints: Move as a unit — stay connected.
Ball-side defender applies pressure, others provide cover.
Don't ball-watch — scan for runners.
Communicate constantly: 'shift left', 'hold', 'step up'.
variations: Add a midfielder to help the back line.
Allow attackers to overlap for realistic scenarios.
Progress to full 8v8 with build-up from the back.

title: HIIT Ball Mastery
instructions: Set up 6 stations in a circle, 5 yards apart.
Station 1: Toe taps on the ball (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Station 2: Ball rolls side-to-side (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Station 3: Around-the-world dribble (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Station 4: Quick feet shuffle with ball (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Station 5: Sprint to cone and back with ball (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Station 6: Juggling (20 seconds on, 10 seconds rest).
Complete 4 full circuits.
coachingPoints: Maximum effort during work periods.
Use rest periods to recover — don't waste them.
Focus on ball control even when tired.
Stay on the balls of your feet.
variations: Extend work periods to 30 seconds.
Reduce rest periods to 5 seconds.
Add partner challenges at each station.
